=

. Prior to stripping temporary support, contractor to confirm with structural engineer minimum

concrete strength requirements. Concrete strength shall not be less than 70 % of design strength .

Concrete strength to be determined by field cured cylinders.

Stripping & reshoring shall be done on same day . Do not leave unreshored slab overnight.

During stripping & reshoring operation, at least one complete bay to be cleared before any reshores are installed.
Place reshores as laid out on plan, denoted by' X ' for #2 post shore TYP.

All equipment must be inspected by contractor prior to use. Bent or damaged equipment should not be used.
Vertical posts or frames must be within 1/2 " of plumb and must be checked prior to concrete placement .
Contractor shall submit reshore plans to designer of the permanent structure for acceptance in accordance
with CSA S269.1 § 7.2.11.

9. Prior to use, inspection by the undersigned P. Eng. is required . ( 48 hrs. notice , please ).

rewritten_text:
## COMPREHENSIVE SHORING AND RESHORING PLAN

**Project Name:** Fraser Crescent
**Project Address:** 32651 Fraser Crescent, Mission, B.C.
**Client/Owner:** (Information not provided, to be confirmed by Contractor)
**General Contractor:** Hamstra Construction Ltd.
**Scaffolding Supplier:** TTF Scaffolding Co. Ltd.
**Scaffolding Supplier Contact:**
*   **Website:** www.ttfscaffolding.com
*   **Telephone:** 778-898-5301
*   **Email:** info@ttfscaffolding.com
**Engineering Firm:** Greuter Engineering Ltd.
**EGBC Permit No.:** 1002364
**Project File No.:** GE-2488-4

---

### 1.0 DRAWING INFORMATION

**Drawing Title:** Level 4 and 5 Suspended Slab Shoring and Level 3 Reshoring Plan for Level 4 Concrete Pour
**Drawing Date:** October 30, 2025
**Drawn By:** F.T.
**Sheet Number:** F-5
**Scales:** As Shown on Plan (Typically 1/8" = 1'-0" unless otherwise noted)
**Revisions:** (Revision log to be maintained by Greuter Engineering Ltd. and TTF Scaffolding Co. Ltd. as per project standards. Date 2025-11-03 noted in original drawing indicates a revision date.)

---

### 2.0 GENERAL NOTES AND COORDINATION REQUIREMENTS

2.1 **Drawing Interpretation:** This drawing is a critical component of the overall construction documentation and **SHALL BE READ IN CONJUNCTION WITH ALL APPLICABLE ARCHITECTURAL AND STRUCTURAL DRAWINGS** for the permanent structure. Any discrepancies or conflicts between these drawings must be immediately brought to the attention of the Project Engineer (P. Eng.) and the General Contractor for resolution prior to commencing any work.

2.2 **Contractor Responsibilities:** The General Contractor is solely responsible for verifying and confirming all dimensions, elevations, and site conditions prior to ordering, receiving, or installing any shoring or reshoring equipment. This includes, but is not limited to, existing slab elevations, column locations, wall lines, and any obstructions. Any discrepancies found must be reported to the Project Engineer (P. Eng.) immediately.

2.3 **Site Conditions:** The design of the shoring and reshoring systems presented herein is based on typical assumed site conditions. The Contractor shall be responsible for assessing and confirming actual site conditions, including soil bearing capacity for ground-supported shoring (if applicable, though these plans focus on suspended slabs), and the structural integrity of existing slabs for reshoring.

2.4 **Cross-Referenced Drawings:**
    *   For General Notes and Typical Details pertaining to shoring and formwork, refer to **Drawing F-1**.
    *   For Reshoring Design and Spacing details specific to Level 2, refer to **Drawing F-4**.

---

### 3.0 STRUCTURAL SPECIFICATIONS - SHORING SYSTEMS

#### 3.1 LEVEL 5 SUSPENDED SLAB SHORING PLAN

This section details the temporary support system required for the casting of the Level 5 suspended concrete slab.

3.1.1 **Slab Elevation and Thickness:**
    *   **Top of Slab Elevation (TOS):** Elevation 92'-9 1/8" (92 feet, 9 and 1/8 inches) above the established project datum. This elevation defines the finished top surface of the Level 5 concrete slab.
    *   **Slab Thickness:** 12 1/2 inches (0.3175 meters). This dimension is crucial for calculating the dead load of the wet concrete and formwork.

3.1.2 **Shoring Height:**
    *   **Typical Shoring Height (H):** 9'-9" (9 feet, 9 inches). This represents the clear vertical distance from the top of the supporting structure (Level 4 slab) to the underside of the Level 5 formwork. This height is critical for determining the required extension of vertical shoring components and ensuring adequate clearance.

3.1.3 **Primary Support Joists/Stringers:**
    *   **Material:** 10-foot (3.05 meters) long, 4-inch by 6-inch (101.6 mm x 152.4 mm nominal) dimensional lumber joists. These members are to be heavy-duty structural grade, such as Douglas Fir #1 or better, free from significant defects that would impair structural integrity.
    *   **Spacing:** Spaced at 16 inches (0.406 meters) on center (O/C). This uniform spacing ensures even distribution of the slab and formwork loads onto the underlying shoring system.
    *   **Function:** These joists serve as the primary horizontal load-carrying members, directly supporting the plywood formwork decking and transferring the wet concrete load to the vertical shoring frames or posts.

3.1.4 **Shoring Frame System:**
    *   **Type:** Heavy-duty modular scaffold frames.
    *   **Dimensions:** 6'-0" (1.83 meters) high by 4'-0" (1.22 meters) wide. These frames are designed for high load applications.
    *   **Load Capacity:** 10 Kips (10,000 pounds or 44.48 kilonewtons) per leg. This is the maximum allowable vertical compressive load that each individual leg of the scaffold frame can safely support. The Contractor must ensure that the actual applied loads do not exceed this capacity, incorporating appropriate safety factors as per engineering design.
    *   **Bracing:** 7'-0" (2.13 meters) diagonal or horizontal bracing, typical unless otherwise noted (U.N.O.). Bracing is essential for ensuring the lateral stability and structural integrity of the shoring tower, preventing buckling and sway.

#### 3.2 LEVEL 4 SUSPENDED SLAB SHORING PLAN

This section details the temporary support system required for the casting of the Level 4 suspended concrete slab.

3.2.1 **Slab Elevations and Thickness:**
    *   **Top of Slab Elevation (TOS) (Higher Section):** Elevation 82'-11 1/2" (82 feet, 11 and 1/2 inches) above the established project datum.
    *   **Top of Slab Elevation (TOS) (Lower Section):** Elevation 82'-6 3/8" (82 feet, 6 and 3/8 inches) above the established project datum. This indicates a stepped slab condition.
    *   **Underside of Slab Elevation:** Elevation 72'-8" (72 feet, 8 inches) above the established project datum. This defines the bottom surface of the Level 4 concrete slab.
    *   **Slab Thickness (Typical):** 11 3/4 inches (0.298 meters). This dimension is crucial for calculating the dead load of the wet concrete and formwork.
    *   **Typical Deep Slab Load:** 194 PSF (Pounds Per Square Foot). This represents the design dead load for the typical 11 1/2-inch deep slab, which includes the weight of the wet concrete and formwork. This value is used in shoring design calculations.

3.2.2 **Shoring Height:**
    *   **Typical Shoring Height (H):** 10'-3 1/2" (10 feet, 3 and 1/2 inches). This represents the clear vertical distance from the top of the supporting structure (Level 3 slab or ground level) to the underside of the Level 4 formwork.

3.2.3 **Primary Support Joists/Stringers:**
    *   **Material:** 10-foot (3.05 meters) long, 4-inch by 6-inch (101.6 mm x 152.4 mm nominal) dimensional lumber joists. These members are to be heavy-duty structural grade, such as Douglas Fir #1 or better.
    *   **Spacing:** Spaced at 16 inches (0.406 meters) on center (O/C). This uniform spacing ensures even distribution of the slab and formwork loads.
    *   **Alternative Spacing:** Some areas may specify 19.2 inches (0.488 meters) on center (O/C) for 10-foot long 4x6 joists, depending on localized load conditions or slab spans. The Contractor must strictly adhere to the spacing indicated on the plan for each specific area.
    *   **Function:** These joists serve as the primary horizontal load-carrying members, directly supporting the plywood formwork decking and transferring the wet concrete load to the vertical shoring frames or posts.

3.2.4 **Shoring Frame System:**
    *   **Type:** Heavy-duty modular scaffold frames, identical to those specified for Level 5.
    *   **Dimensions:** 6'-0" (1.83 meters) high by 4'-0" (1.22 meters) wide.
    *   **Load Capacity:** 10 Kips (10,000 pounds or 44.48 kilonewtons) per leg.
    *   **Bracing:** 7'-0" (2.13 meters) diagonal or horizontal bracing, typical unless otherwise noted (U.N.O.).

3.2.5 **Slab Step Conditions:**
    *   **1/8" Step:** Indicates a minor change in elevation or thickness of the slab or formwork. This small step must be accurately formed to maintain the design slab profile.
    *   **5 1/2" Step:** Indicates a more significant change in elevation of the slab or formwork. This step will require specific forming details and potentially localized adjustments to shoring heights to maintain proper support.

3.2.6 **Typical Bay Dimensions:**
    *   **16'-0" Bay:** Indicates a typical bay or span dimension of 16 feet (4.88 meters) for the shoring layout. This dimension helps define the grid for placing shoring frames and beams.

3.2.7 **Post Shore Requirements:**
    *   **General Rule:** No shoring is typically required directly under the slab unless specifically shown or marked with an "X" on the plan.
    *   **Designated Locations:** Locations marked with an "X" denote areas where #2 Post Shores are required. The plan view must be consulted for the precise placement of these individual post shores.
    *   **Function:** Post shores provide concentrated vertical support where modular frames may not be feasible or where additional localized support is required due to specific slab conditions or load concentrations.

---

### 4.0 STRUCTURAL SPECIFICATIONS - RESHORING SYSTEMS

#### 4.1 LEVEL 3 SUSPENDED SLAB RESHORING PLAN

Reshoring is the process of providing temporary support to a newly stripped concrete slab to enable it to carry construction loads from subsequent levels above, ensuring that the slab is not overstressed before it achieves its full design strength.

4.1.1 **Reshoring Design and Spacing:**
    *   For detailed reshoring design and spacing information specific to Level 2, refer to **Drawing F-4**. This drawing provides the comprehensive layout and specifications for the reshoring system at that level.

4.1.2 **Reshoring Components - Tube and Clamp System:**
    *   **Purpose:** Used for framing and bracing of vertical shoring legs, providing lateral stability and load transfer.
    *   **Vertical Framing:**
        *   **Material:** 2-inch (50.8 mm) nominal diameter steel tube and clamp system.
        *   **Length:** 15 feet (4.57 meters) long tubes.
        *   **Quantity:** 8 pieces (PCS) are specified for vertical framing.
        *   **Levels:** To be installed at two distinct levels: 12 feet (3.66 meters) high and 24 feet (7.32 meters) high. These heights refer to the vertical position of the horizontal tube members relative to the base of the shoring system.
    *   **Horizontal Framing/Bracing:**
        *   **Material:** 2-inch (50.8 mm) nominal diameter steel tube and clamp system.
        *   **Lengths:** A combination of 12 feet (3.66 meters) long and 10 feet (3.05 meters) long tubes.
        *   **Quantity:** 8 pieces (PCS) total are specified for horizontal framing/bracing.
        *   **Levels:** To be installed at two distinct levels: 12 feet (3.66 meters) high and 24 feet (7.32 meters) high. These horizontal members connect the vertical shoring elements, forming a rigid and stable framework.

4.1.3 **Post Shore Specifications for Reshoring:**
    *   **Type:** #2 Post Shores. These are heavy-duty adjustable steel shores commonly used for reshoring applications.
    *   **Designation:** Denoted by an "X" on the plan view. The Contractor must place #2 Post Shores precisely at these marked locations.
    *   **Grid Layout:** Typically placed on a 6-foot by 6-foot (1.83 m x 1.83 m) maximum grid, unless otherwise noted (U.N.O.). This grid ensures adequate and uniform support for the reshoring slab.
    *   **Capacity:** Each #2 Post Shore has a rated capacity of 10,100 pounds (45.81 kilonewtons) when extended to a height of 9 feet (2.74 meters). The load capacity of adjustable post shores can vary significantly with extension height; the Contractor must ensure that the actual height of installation does not exceed the rated height for the specified capacity.

#### 4.2 RESHORING DIAGRAM (VERTICAL SECTION)

This diagram illustrates the load distribution and required reshoring percentages for consecutive concrete pours, ensuring that the supporting slabs are not overloaded. The percentages indicate the proportion of the total load from the wet concrete and formwork above that must be carried by the reshoring system at each level.

*   **Level 4 (Pour Level):**
    *   **Slab Thickness:** 17 inches (0.432 meters).
    *   **Concrete Strength Reached:** The concrete in the newly poured Level 4 slab must achieve a specified strength, indicated by a load capacity of 262 PSF (Pounds Per Square Foot), before any load is transferred to it by stripping the formwork. This strength is typically a percentage of the design strength, confirmed by field-cured cylinders.
    *   **Support:** This level is initially supported by full formwork and shoring (indicated by "F").

*   **Level 3 (Reshored Level):**
    *   **Reshoring Requirement:** 75% (seventy-five percent) of the construction load from Level 4 must be transferred through reshores (indicated by "R") to Level 3. This means Level 3 must be capable of supporting 75% of the wet concrete and formwork load from Level 4, in addition to its own dead load and any other existing loads.

*   **Level 2 (Reshored Level):**
    *   **Reshoring Requirement:** 40% (forty percent) of the construction load from Level 4 must be transferred through reshores (indicated by "R") to Level 2. This load is cumulative with loads transferred from Level 3.

*   **Level 1 (Reshored Level):**
    *   **Reshoring Requirement:** 100% (one hundred percent) of the construction load from Level 4 must be transferred through reshores (indicated by "R") to Level 1. This implies that Level 1 is the primary load-bearing level for the accumulated construction loads from above.

*   **Parkade Level (Lower Level Reshore - LKR):**
    *   **Reshoring Requirement:** 100% (one hundred percent) of the construction load from Level 4 must be transferred through reshores (indicated by "R") to the Parkade Level. This indicates that the Parkade slab is designed to act as the ultimate temporary support for the entire shoring/reshoring stack.

---

### 5.0 MATERIAL REQUIREMENTS

5.1 **Lumber:**
    *   **Type:** Dimensional Lumber Joists/Stringers (e.g., 4x6).
    *   **Grade:** Minimum Douglas Fir #1 or equivalent heavy-duty structural grade. All lumber must be free from significant defects such as large knots, splits, or excessive wane that would compromise its structural integrity.
    *   **Condition:** All lumber must be new or in excellent condition, straight, and free of rot, insect damage, or excessive moisture content.

5.2 **Plywood:**
    *   **Type:** Formwork Decking Plywood.
    *   **Grade:** Minimum 3/4-inch (19 mm) thick, Douglas Fir #1 or better. This grade ensures adequate strength and stiffness for the formwork surface and provides a smooth finish to the concrete.
    *   **Condition:** Plywood must be in good condition, free from delamination, excessive warping, or damage that would affect the quality of the concrete finish or structural performance.

5.3 **Scaffolding Frames:**
    *   **Type:** Heavy-Duty Modular Steel Scaffold Frames.
    *   **Dimensions:** 6'-0" high x 4'-0" wide (1.83 m x 1.22 m).
    *   **Capacity:** Rated for 10 Kips (10,000 lbs) per leg.
    *   **Bracing:** 7'-0" (2.13 m) diagonal or horizontal bracing.
    *   **Standards:** All frames and components must conform to applicable industry standards (e.g., CSA S269.2, ANSI/ASSE A10.8) and manufacturer's specifications.

5.4 **Tube and Clamp Components:**
    *   **Type:** Steel Tube and Clamp Scaffolding System.
    *   **Tube Diameter:** 2-inch (50.8 mm) nominal outside diameter steel tubing.
    *   **Clamps:** Heavy-duty forged or pressed steel couplers (right-angle, swivel, and sleeve couplers) suitable for structural connections.
    *   **Standards:** All tubes and clamps must conform to applicable industry standards (e.g., CSA S269.2, EN 12811) and manufacturer's specifications, ensuring proper load transfer and connection integrity.

5.5 **Post Shores:**
    *   **Type:** #2 Adjustable Steel Post Shores.
    *   **Capacity:** Rated for 10,100 lbs (45.81 kN) at a 9'-0" (2.74 m) extension height.
    *   **Condition:** Must be in excellent working order, free from bends, dents, or corrosion that would impair their load-carrying capacity or adjustment mechanism. Locking pins and base/head

**Project Address:** 32651 Fraser Crescent, Mission, B.C.

*   **Telephone:** 778-898-5301
*   **Email:** info@ttfscaffolding.com
**Engineering Firm:** Greuter Engineering Ltd.
**EGBC Permit No.:** 1002364
**Project File No.:** GE-2488-4

---

### 1.0 DRAWING INFORMATION

**Drawing Title:** Level 4 and 5 Suspended Slab Shoring and Level 3 Reshoring Plan for Level 4 Concrete Pour
**Drawing Date:** October 30, 2025
**Drawn By:** F.T.
**Sheet Number:** F-5
**Scales:** As Shown on Plan (Typically 1/8" = 1'-0" unless otherwise noted)
**Revisions:** (Revision log to be maintained by Greuter Engineering Ltd. and TTF Scaffolding Co. Ltd. as per project standards. Date 2025-11-03 noted in original drawing indicates a revision date.)

3.2.5 **Slab Step Conditions:**
    *   **1/8" Step:** Indicates a minor change in elevation or thickness of the slab or formwork. This small step must be accurately formed to maintain the design slab profile.
    *   **5 1/2" Step:** Indicates a more significant change in elevation of the slab or formwork. This step will require specific forming details and potentially localized adjustments to shoring heights to maintain proper support.

3.2.6 **Typical Bay Dimensions:**
    *   **16'-0" Bay:** Indicates a typical bay or span dimension of 16 feet (4.88 meters) for the shoring layout. This dimension helps define the grid for placing shoring frames and beams.

3.2.7 **Post Shore Requirements:**
    *   **General Rule:** No shoring is typically required directly under the slab unless specifically shown or marked with an "X" on the plan.
    *   **Designated Locations:** Locations marked with an "X" denote areas where #2 Post Shores are required. The plan view must be consulted for the precise placement of these individual post shores.
    *   **Function:** Post shores provide concentrated vertical support where modular frames may not be feasible or where additional localized support is required due to specific slab conditions or load concentrations.

---

### 4.0 STRUCTURAL SPECIFICATIONS - RESHORING SYSTEMS

#### 4.1 LEVEL 3 SUSPENDED SLAB RESHORING PLAN

Reshoring is the process of providing temporary support to a newly stripped concrete slab to enable it to carry construction loads from subsequent levels above, ensuring that the slab is not overstressed before it achieves its full design strength.

4.1.1 **Reshoring Design and Spacing:**
    *   For detailed reshoring design and spacing information specific to Level 2, refer to **Drawing F-4**. This drawing provides the comprehensive layout and specifications for the reshoring system at that level.

4.1.2 **Reshoring Components - Tube and Clamp System:**
    *   **Purpose:** Used for framing and bracing of vertical shoring legs, providing lateral stability and load transfer.
    *   **Vertical Framing:**
        *   **Material:** 2-inch (50.8 mm) nominal diameter steel tube and clamp system.
        *   **Length:** 15 feet (4.57 meters) long tubes.
        *   **Quantity:** 8 pieces (PCS) are specified for vertical framing.
        *   **Levels:** To be installed at two distinct levels: 12 feet (3.66 meters) high and 24 feet (7.32 meters) high. These heights refer to the vertical position of the horizontal tube members relative to the base of the shoring system.
    *   **Horizontal Framing/Bracing:**
        *   **Material:** 2-inch (50.8 mm) nominal diameter steel tube and clamp system.
        *   **Lengths:** A combination of 12 feet (3.66 meters) long and 10 feet (3.05 meters) long tubes.
        *   **Quantity:** 8 pieces (PCS) total are specified for horizontal framing/bracing.
        *   **Levels:** To be installed at two distinct levels: 12 feet (3.66 meters) high and 24 feet (7.32 meters) high. These horizontal members connect the vertical shoring elements, forming a rigid and stable framework.

4.1.3 **Post Shore Specifications for Reshoring:**
    *   **Type:** #2 Post Shores. These are heavy-duty adjustable steel shores commonly used for reshoring applications.
    *   **Designation:** Denoted by an "X" on the plan view. The Contractor must place #2 Post Shores precisely at these marked locations.
    *   **Grid Layout:** Typically placed on a 6-foot by 6-foot (1.83 m x 1.83 m) maximum grid, unless otherwise noted (U.N.O.). This grid ensures adequate and uniform support for the reshoring slab.
    *   **Capacity:** Each #2 Post Shore has a rated capacity of 10,100 pounds (45.81 kilonewtons) when extended to a height of 9 feet (2.74 meters). The load capacity of adjustable post shores can vary significantly with extension height; the Contractor must ensure that the actual height of installation does not exceed the rated height for the specified capacity.

#### 4.2 RESHORING DIAGRAM (VERTICAL SECTION)

This diagram illustrates the load distribution and required reshoring percentages for consecutive concrete pours, ensuring that the supporting slabs are not overloaded. The percentages indicate the proportion of the total load from the wet concrete and formwork above that must be carried by the reshoring system at each level.

*   **Level 4 (Pour Level):**
    *   **Slab Thickness:** 17 inches (0.432 meters).
    *   **Concrete Strength Reached:** The concrete in the newly poured Level 4 slab must achieve a specified strength, indicated by a load capacity of 262 PSF (Pounds Per Square Foot), before any load is transferred to it by stripping the formwork. This strength is typically a percentage of the design strength, confirmed by field-cured cylinders.
    *   **Support:** This level is initially supported by full formwork and shoring (indicated by "F").

*   **Level 3 (Reshored Level):**
    *   **Reshoring Requirement:** 75% (seventy-five percent) of the construction load from Level 4 must be transferred through reshores (indicated by "R") to Level 3. This means Level 3 must be capable of supporting 75% of the wet concrete and formwork load from Level 4, in addition to its own dead load and any other existing loads.

*   **Level 2 (Reshored Level):**
    *   **Reshoring Requirement:** 40% (forty percent) of the construction load from Level 4 must be transferred through reshores (indicated by "R") to Level 2. This load is cumulative with loads transferred from Level 3.

*   **Level 1 (Reshored Level):**
    *   **Reshoring Requirement:** 100% (one hundred percent) of the construction load from Level 4 must be transferred through reshores (indicated by "R") to Level 1. This implies that Level 1 is the primary load-bearing level for the accumulated construction loads from above.

*   **Parkade Level (Lower Level Reshore - LKR):**
    *   **Reshoring Requirement:** 100% (one hundred percent) of the construction load from Level 4 must be transferred through reshores (indicated by "R") to the Parkade Level. This indicates that the Parkade slab is designed to act as the ultimate temporary support for the entire shoring/reshoring stack.

---

### 5.0 MATERIAL REQUIREMENTS

5.1 **Lumber:**
    *   **Type:** Dimensional Lumber Joists/Stringers (e.g., 4x6).
    *   **Grade:** Minimum Douglas Fir #1 or equivalent heavy-duty structural grade. All lumber must be free from significant defects such as large knots, splits, or excessive wane that would compromise its structural integrity.
    *   **Condition:** All lumber must be new or in excellent condition, straight, and free of rot, insect damage, or excessive moisture content.

5.2 **Plywood:**
    *   **Type:** Formwork Decking Plywood.
    *   **Grade:** Minimum 3/4-inch (19 mm) thick, Douglas Fir #1 or better. This grade ensures adequate strength and stiffness for the formwork surface and provides a smooth finish to the concrete.
    *   **Condition:** Plywood must be in good condition, free from delamination, excessive warping, or damage that would affect the quality of the concrete finish or structural performance.

5.3 **Scaffolding Frames:**
    *   **Type:** Heavy-Duty Modular Steel Scaffold Frames.
    *   **Dimensions:** 6'-0" high x 4'-0" wide (1.83 m x 1.22 m).
    *   **Capacity:** Rated for 10 Kips (10,000 lbs) per leg.
    *   **Bracing:** 7'-0" (2.13 m) diagonal or horizontal bracing.
    *   **Standards:** All frames and components must conform to applicable industry standards (e.g., CSA S269.2, ANSI/ASSE A10.8) and manufacturer's specifications.

5.4 **Tube and Clamp Components:**
    *   **Type:** Steel Tube and Clamp Scaffolding System.
    *   **Tube Diameter:** 2-inch (50.8 mm) nominal outside diameter steel tubing.
    *   **Clamps:** Heavy-duty forged or pressed steel couplers (right-angle, swivel, and sleeve couplers) suitable for structural connections.
    *   **Standards:** All tubes and clamps must conform to applicable industry standards (e.g., CSA S269.2, EN 12811) and manufacturer's specifications, ensuring proper load transfer and connection integrity.

5.5 **Post Shores:**
    *   **Type:** #2 Adjustable Steel Post Shores.
    *   **Capacity:** Rated for 10,100 lbs (45.81 kN) at a 9'-0" (2.74 m) extension height.
    *   **Condition:** Must be in excellent working order, free from bends, dents, or corrosion that would impair their load-carrying capacity or adjustment mechanism. Locking pins and base/head

**Client/Owner:** (Information not provided, to be confirmed by Contractor)
**Drawing Title:** Level 4 and 5 Suspended Slab Shoring and Level 3 Reshoring Plan for Level 4 Concrete Pour
**Scales:** As Shown on Plan (Typically 1/8" = 1'-0" unless otherwise noted)
**Revisions:** (Revision log to be maintained by Greuter Engineering Ltd. and TTF Scaffolding Co. Ltd. as per project standards. Date 2025-11-03 noted in original drawing indicates a revision date.)

2.1 **Drawing Interpretation:** This drawing is a critical component of the overall construction documentation and **SHALL BE READ IN CONJUNCTION WITH ALL APPLICABLE ARCHITECTURAL AND STRUCTURAL DRAWINGS** for the permanent structure. Any discrepancies or conflicts between these drawings must be immediately brought to the attention of the Project Engineer (P. Eng.) and the General Contractor for resolution prior to commencing any work.

2.2 **Contractor Responsibilities:** The General Contractor is solely responsible for verifying and confirming all dimensions, elevations, and site conditions prior to ordering, receiving, or installing any shoring or reshoring equipment. This includes, but is not limited to, existing slab elevations, column locations, wall lines, and any obstructions. Any discrepancies found must be reported to the Project Engineer (P. Eng.) immediately.

2.3 **Site Conditions:** The design of the shoring and reshoring systems presented herein is based on typical assumed site conditions. The Contractor shall be responsible for assessing and confirming actual site conditions, including soil bearing capacity for ground-supported shoring (if applicable, though these plans focus on suspended slabs), and the structural integrity of existing slabs for reshoring.
*   For General Notes and Typical Details pertaining to shoring and formwork, refer to **Drawing F-1**.
*   For Reshoring Design and Spacing details specific to Level 2, refer to **Drawing F-4**.
This section details the temporary support system required for the casting of the Level 5 suspended concrete slab.

*   **Top of Slab Elevation (TOS):** Elevation 92'-9 1/8" (92 feet, 9 and 1/8 inches) above the established project datum. This elevation defines the finished top surface of the Level 5 concrete slab.
*   **Slab Thickness:** 12 1/2 inches (0.3175 meters). This dimension is crucial for calculating the dead load of the wet concrete and formwork.

*   **Typical Shoring Height (H):** 9'-9" (9 feet, 9 inches). This represents the clear vertical distance from the top of the supporting structure (Level 4 slab) to the underside of the Level 5 formwork. This height is critical for determining the required extension of vertical shoring components and ensuring adequate clearance.

*   **Material:** 10-foot (3.05 meters) long, 4-inch by 6-inch (101.6 mm x 152.4 mm nominal) dimensional lumber joists. These members are to be heavy-duty structural grade, such as Douglas Fir #1 or better, free from significant defects that would impair structural integrity.
*   **Spacing:** Spaced at 16 inches (0.406 meters) on center (O/C). This uniform spacing ensures even distribution of the slab and formwork loads onto the underlying shoring system.

*   **Function:** These joists serve as the primary horizontal load-carrying members, directly supporting the plywood formwork decking and transferring the wet concrete load to the vertical shoring frames or posts.
*   **Dimensions:** 6'-0" (1.83 meters) high by 4'-0" (1.22 meters) wide. These frames are designed for high load applications.

*   **Load Capacity:** 10 Kips (10,000 pounds or 44.48 kilonewtons) per leg. This is the maximum allowable vertical compressive load that each individual leg of the scaffold frame can safely support. The Contractor must ensure that the actual applied loads do not exceed this capacity, incorporating appropriate safety factors as per engineering design.

*   **Bracing:** 7'-0" (2.13 meters) diagonal or horizontal bracing, typical unless otherwise noted (U.N.O.). Bracing is essential for ensuring the lateral stability and structural integrity of the shoring tower, preventing buckling and sway.
This section details the temporary support system required for the casting of the Level 4 suspended concrete slab.
*   **Top of Slab Elevation (TOS) (Higher Section):** Elevation 82'-11 1/2" (82 feet, 11 and 1/2 inches) above the established project datum.
*   **Top of Slab Elevation (TOS) (Lower Section):** Elevation 82'-6 3/8" (82 feet, 6 and 3/8 inches) above the established project datum. This indicates a stepped slab condition.
*   **Underside of Slab Elevation:** Elevation 72'-8" (72 feet, 8 inches) above the established project datum. This defines the bottom surface of the Level 4 concrete slab.
*   **Slab Thickness (Typical):** 11 3/4 inches (0.298 meters). This dimension is crucial for calculating the dead load of the wet concrete and formwork.

*   **Typical Deep Slab Load:** 194 PSF (Pounds Per Square Foot). This represents the design dead load for the typical 11 1/2-inch deep slab, which includes the weight of the wet concrete and formwork. This value is used in shoring design calculations.

*   **Typical Shoring Height (H):** 10'-3 1/2" (10 feet, 3 and 1/2 inches). This represents the clear vertical distance from the top of the supporting structure (Level 3 slab or ground level) to the underside of the Level 4 formwork.

*   **Material:** 10-foot (3.05 meters) long, 4-inch by 6-inch (101.6 mm x 152.4 mm nominal) dimensional lumber joists. These members are to be heavy-duty structural grade, such as Douglas Fir #1 or better.
*   **Spacing:** Spaced at 16 inches (0.406 meters) on center (O/C). This uniform spacing ensures even distribution of the slab and formwork loads.

*   **Alternative Spacing:** Some areas may specify 19.2 inches (0.488 meters) on center (O/C) for 10-foot long 4x6 joists, depending on localized load conditions or slab spans. The Contractor must strictly adhere to the spacing indicated on the plan for each specific area.

*   **Function:** These joists serve as the primary horizontal load-carrying members, directly supporting the plywood formwork decking and transferring the wet concrete load to the vertical shoring frames or posts.
*   **Type:** Heavy-duty modular scaffold frames, identical to those specified for Level 5.
*   **Load Capacity:** 10 Kips (10,000 pounds or 44.48 kilonewtons) per leg.
*   **Bracing:** 7'-0" (2.13 meters) diagonal or horizontal bracing, typical unless otherwise noted (U.N.O.).
*   **1/8" Step:** Indicates a minor change in elevation or thickness of the slab or formwork. This small step must be accurately formed to maintain the design slab profile.

*   **5 1/2" Step:** Indicates a more significant change in elevation of the slab or formwork. This step will require specific forming details and potentially localized adjustments to shoring heights to maintain proper support.
*   **16'-0" Bay:** Indicates a typical bay or span dimension of 16 feet (4.88 meters) for the shoring layout. This dimension helps define the grid for placing shoring frames and beams.
*   **General Rule:** No shoring is typically required directly under the slab unless specifically shown or marked with an "X" on the plan.
*   **Designated Locations:** Locations marked with an "X" denote areas where #2 Post Shores are required. The plan view must be consulted for the precise placement of these individual post shores.

*   **Function:** Post shores provide concentrated vertical support where modular frames may not be feasible or where additional localized support is required due to specific slab conditions or load concentrations.
